title: Basic
summary: Models a conformal class as a bundle of metric rays with a free and transitive positive-dilation action, then introduces scales and the comparison factor between scales.

mathematicalContent.heading: Geometric model
mathematicalContent.body: Fix a base space M. For each x in M, let P_x be the type of concrete metrics at x and let C_x be the abstract conformal ray over x. The Lean structure requires positive dilations on C_x to act freely and transitively. It also supplies dilation on P_x and requires projection from concrete metrics to conformal rays to commute with dilation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Bundles and scales
mathematicalContent.body: The concrete metric bundle and the conformal metric bundle are dependent sums over M. A scale chooses a concrete metric field g_s and the corresponding section s of the conformal ray bundle. Relative scale factors record the unique positive dilation relating two such sections.
mathematicalContent.formula: \widetilde{\mathcal P}=\coprod_{x\in M}\mathcal P_x,\qquad \mathcal G=\coprod_{x\in M}\mathcal C_x,\qquad \delta_{\lambda_{s_1,s_2}(x)}s_1(x)=s_2(x)

mathematicalContent.heading: Notation from header.tex
mathematicalContent.body: The uploaded shortcuts are interpreted mathematically here: R denotes the real numbers, Met denotes the metric structure, and Conf denotes the conformal structure. The displayed formulas use their expanded standard LaTeX forms so KaTeX does not depend on private TeX macros.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The multiplicative group of units in the nonnegative real numbers. These are exactly the strictly positive real scaling factors.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev PosReal := Units NNReal

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A metric structure assigns to every point x a type P_x of concrete pointwise metrics.
correspondence.leanType: class MetricStructure (M : Type u) where PointMetric : M → Type v

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A global metric field is a section that chooses one pointwise metric g(x) in P_x for every x.
correspondence.leanType: def MetricField := ∀ x : M, Met.PointMetric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An abstract conformal structure consists of conformal metric fibers C_x, a projection from concrete metrics, and compatible positive-dilation actions. The action on each C_x is free and transitive, so every two elements of one fiber differ by a unique positive factor.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\lbrack g\rbrack&\in\mathcal C_x, & \delta_a&:\mathcal C_x\to\mathcal C_x,\\ \delta_1&=\operatorname{id}, & \delta_{ab}&=\delta_a\circ\delta_b,\\ \forall g,h\in\mathcal C_x,&\exists!a\in\mathbb R_{>0}:\delta_ag=h,\\ \delta_a^{\mathcal P}&:\mathcal P_x\to\mathcal P_x, & \lbrack\delta_a^{\mathcal P}g\rbrack&=\delta_a\lbrack g\rbrack.\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure ConformalStructure (M : Type u) extends MetricStructure M

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At x, PointwiseMetric is simply the concrete metric type supplied by the conformal structure.
correspondence.leanType: def PointwiseMetric (x : M) := Conf.PointMetric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A metric field for Conf assigns a concrete metric in P_x to each point x.
correspondence.leanType: def MetricField := ∀ x : M, Conf.PointMetric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The total space of concrete pointwise metrics is the disjoint union of the fibers P_x.
correspondence.leanType: def PointMetricBundle := Σ x : M, Conf.PointMetric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The conformal metric bundle is the disjoint union of the metric-ray fibers C_x.
correspondence.leanType: def MetricBundle := Σ x : M, Conf.MetricFiber x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Projection of the conformal metric bundle onto its base point.
correspondence.leanType: def proj (u : MetricBundle Conf) : M := u.1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Projection of the concrete metric bundle onto its base point.
correspondence.leanType: def pointproj (u : PointMetricBundle Conf) : M := u.1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Positive dilation acts vertically on the conformal metric bundle: the base point stays fixed and only the fiber coordinate is dilated.
correspondence.leanType: def dilateBundle (r : PosReal) : Conf.MetricBundle → Conf.MetricBundle

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Concrete metric dilation acts vertically on the concrete metric bundle.
correspondence.leanType: def dilatePointMetricBundle (a : PosReal) : Conf.PointMetricBundle → Conf.PointMetricBundle

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A scale chooses a concrete global metric g_s and a point in every conformal ray, with the compatibility condition that the chosen ray is the image of g_s.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: s=(g_s,\widehat s),\qquad g_s(x)\in\mathcal P_x,\quad\widehat s(x)\in\mathcal C_x,\quad[g_s(x)]=\widehat s(x)
correspondence.leanType: structure Scale where totalmetric : Conf.MetricField; metric : ∀ x : M, Conf.MetricFiber x; compat : ∀ x : M, Conf.toFiber (totalmetric x) = metric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A scale determines a section of the conformal metric bundle.
correspondence.leanType: def Scale.section (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) : M → ConformalStructure.MetricBundle Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a chosen scale s and a fiber element g over x, scaleFactor is the unique positive number that dilates s(x) to g. Lean selects the witness supplied by transitivity.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def scaleFactor (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) {x : M} (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : PosReal

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A scale trivializes the conformal metric bundle by recording the positive fiber coordinate and the base point.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def trivialization (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) : Conf.MetricBundle ≃ PosReal × M

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A chosen scale supplies an element s(x) in every conformal metric fiber, so each fiber is nonempty.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fiber_nonempty (s : ConformalStructure.Scale Conf) (x : M) : Nonempty (Conf.MetricFiber x)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The relative factor from s₁ to s₂ is the scale factor of s₂(x) measured relative to s₁.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) : M → PosReal

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The second scale is recovered pointwise by dilating the first scale with their relative factor.
correspondence.leanType: theorem scale_eq_dilate_relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) : s₂.metric = fun x => Conf.dilate (Conf.relativeFactor s₁ s₂ x) (s₁.metric x)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The concrete metric bundle maps to the conformal metric bundle by replacing a concrete metric with its ray class.
correspondence.leanType: def pointToFiberBundle : Conf.PointMetricBundle → Conf.MetricBundle

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A positive function σ rescales a concrete metric field pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def dilateMetricField (σ : M → PosReal) (g : Conf.MetricField) : Conf.MetricField

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A constant positive number a rescales the metric field by the same factor at every point.
correspondence.leanType: def dilateMetricFieldConst (a : PosReal) (g : Conf.MetricField) : Conf.MetricField

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constant dilation of a scale rescales both its concrete representative and its conformal-ray section; compatibility follows from equivariance of toFiber.
correspondence.leanType: def Scale.dilateTotalMetric (s : Conf.Scale) (a : PosReal) : Conf.Scale

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, metric bundle and homogeneous structures
references.note: Background for viewing a conformal class through its positive metric ray and the associated dilation bundle.

limitations: This file formalizes an abstract algebraic skeleton: MetricFiber is not yet constructed as the ray of positive-definite symmetric bilinear forms on a manifold. Lean verifies every displayed identity under the fields and axioms of ConformalStructure.
